title: Getting random 403 Forbidden errors on the dashboard
body: I keep getting 403 errors when I try to access the TaskFlow dashboard. It's not every time — sometimes it loads fine, sometimes I get a 403 page. It seems totally random. My permissions haven't changed and other people on my team say they're seeing it too. Started happening a couple days ago.

ground_truth.description: The reporter is a regular user. The company runs TaskFlow behind a load balancer that round-robins across 3 application servers. Two days ago, a deployment went out to update the RBAC (role-based access control) policy to add a new 'analyst' role. The deployment succeeded on servers 1 and 2 but failed on server 3 due to a disk space issue — the deploy script exited silently without rolling back. Server 3 is running the old RBAC policy that doesn't recognize the 'analyst' role. Users with the 'analyst' role get 403 on every request that hits server 3 (roughly 1 in 3 requests). Users with older roles (admin, editor, viewer) are unaffected. The reporter was recently assigned the 'analyst' role. The reporter has noticed it's roughly 1-in-3 but attributes it to 'network issues.'
ground_truth.actual_cause: One of three load-balanced servers has a stale deployment with an old RBAC policy that doesn't include the recently-added 'analyst' role. Requests that round-robin to this server return 403 for users with the analyst role. The fix is to redeploy to server 3 or fix the disk space issue and re-run the deployment.
acceptable_paths: Identifying the 1-in-3 pattern and suspecting a load balancer or inconsistent server state, even without identifying the RBAC policy as the specific issue
Identifying that only users with the 'analyst' role are affected and connecting it to the recent role addition, even without discovering the stale server
ground_truth.environment: TaskFlow v2.3.1, 3 load-balanced servers, Nginx load balancer, recently added 'analyst' RBAC role
ground_truth.workaround: Refresh the page until you get a working server; or temporarily reassign affected users to the 'editor' role

expected_triage_extracts: 403 errors are intermittent, roughly 1 in 3 requests
coincides with a recent deployment or role change
affects users with the 'analyst' role specifically
other roles (admin, editor, viewer) are unaffected
